posts.raw_content:
God reveals to us how to receive His blessings in His Holy Word

(God blesses His people) “Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men! 9 For he satisfies the longing soul, and fills the hungry soul with goodness. (Then people are deceived and not grateful) 10 Such as sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, being bound in affliction and iron; 11 Because they rebelled against the words of God, and contemned [despised / scorned] the counsel of the most High: 12 Therefore he brought down their heart with labor; they fell down, and there was none to help. [God hears the peoples pray and rescues them] 13 Then they cried unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses. 14 He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke their bands in sunder. [God wants His people to respond to His love for them] 15 Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men! 16 For he has broken the gates of brass, and cut the bars of iron in sunder. [The deception returns] 17 Fools because of their transgression, and because of their iniquities, are afflicted. 18 Their soul abhorres all manner of meat; and they draw near unto the gates of death. [God’s people return to their Creator once again] 19 Then they cry unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saves them out of their distresses.  [The method God uses to rescue His people] 20 He sent his word, and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions.” (Psalms 107:8-20)

“Faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.” (Romans 10:17)

“Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and comes down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.” (James 1:17)

(God blesses His people) “Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men! 9 For he satisfies the longing soul, and fills the hungry soul with goodness. (Then people are deceived and not grateful) 10 Such as sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, being bound in affliction and iron; 11 Because they rebelled against the words of God, and contemned [despised / scorned] the counsel of the most High: 12 Therefore he brought down their heart with labor; they fell down, and there was none to help. [God hears the peoples pray and rescues them] 13 Then they cried unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses. 14 He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke their bands in sunder. [God wants His people to respond to His love for them] 15 Oh that men would praise the Lord for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men! 16 For he has broken the gates of br ass, and cut the bars of iron in sunder. [The deception returns] 17 Fools because of their transgression, and because of their iniquities, are afflicted. 18 Their soul abhorres all manner of meat; and they draw near unto the gates of death. [The method God uses to rescue His people] 20 He sent his word, and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions.” (Psalms 107:8-20)
